summary: Backend regression suite (28 tests) for THE LIONEYO. 26 passed, 2 failed. All seed data, public catalogue, coupons, admin auth/CRUD (products/collections/coupons/settings/reviews), analytics, order create (prepaid + partial_cod with payable_now=150), Razorpay live order_id generation, signature verification, order tracking, wishlist toggle, review creation, and Google OAuth 401 path all verified end-to-end against the public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL.

critical.issue: Returns 500 'ObjectId is not iterable'. motor insert_one() mutates the in-memory doc and injects a BSON ObjectId into `_id`; the same dict is then returned and FastAPI fails to JSON-serialise it. Fix: pop `_id` before returning OR return Product(**doc).model_dump().

Route is registered on `app` root, not under `/api`. Kubernetes ingress only routes `/api/*` to the backend so external requests hit the React frontend (HTML returned instead of XML). Move under /api/sitemap.xml or expose via ingress. /robots.txt has the same root-level routing problem but accidentally returns 200 because the frontend bundles its own public/robots.txt.

action_items: Fix duplicate_product handler in /app/backend/server.py (line ~222): exclude `_id` before returning the freshly inserted doc (e.g., doc.pop('_id', None) after db.products.insert_one or use Product(**doc).model_dump()).
Expose /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt under /api/ prefix (or update ingress) so external SEO crawlers actually reach the backend instead of the SPA HTML.
critical_code_review_comments: ObjectId leak pattern recurs anywhere we re-insert a previously-fetched doc (e.g., duplicate_product). Audit other handlers (none currently bitten, but pattern is fragile).
RAZORPAY LIVE keys are committed in /app/backend/.env. Acceptable for this iteration but should be rotated/secret-managed before public deploy.

/app/backend/server.py is ~660 lines and mixes auth, catalogue, orders, payments, OAuth, uploads, analytics, SEO. Recommend splitting into routers (admin.py, orders.py, catalogue.py, auth.py) before file growth continues.
settings doc still returns `_id` only because of the pop in /admin/settings — `get_settings_doc` should always project `{_id:0}` consistently to avoid future regressions.
Order creation calls live Razorpay synchronously inside the request — consider try/except + idempotency to avoid duplicate orders if the client retries.
seed_data_creation: Used auto-seeded data only (3 collections, 8 products, 3 coupons). Test artefacts use TEST_ prefix and are cleaned up by each test.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_lioneyo_backend.py after main agent fixes the duplicate handler and sitemap routing. All other 26 endpoints are healthy. Razorpay LIVE order creation works and returns valid `order_xxxx` ids — do NOT proceed to actual payment capture during retest.
rca of the issue: 1) duplicate_product: motor's insert_one() mutates `doc` in-place adding BSON `_id`. Returning that dict triggers FastAPI's jsonable_encoder which can't serialise ObjectId. Repro: POST /api/admin/products/{any-existing-id}/duplicate with admin Bearer token. Mitigation: `doc.pop('_id', None)` after insert OR rebuild via Product(**doc).model_dump(). 2) sitemap.xml: route declared with @app.get not @api.get; ingress routes only `/api/*` to backend port 8001, so external GET /sitemap.xml is served by the React dev server (returns index.html).
